reasoning:
Since I do not have access to search tools to check the specific 10-day forecast for October 26, 2025, I am relying on the climatology of Bordeaux-Mérignac. The average daily temperature in Bordeaux drops significantly from late October through January. 
- Late October (Oct 26) average daily mean is roughly 13°C.
- Early November (Nov 2) average is ~12°C.
- Late November (Nov 25) average is ~8.5°C.
- Late January (Jan 24) average is ~6.5°C.

Assuming daily temperature standard deviations of 3.5-4.5°C, the difference D = T_future - T_Oct26 follows a normal distribution where the expected difference becomes increasingly negative as we move deeper into winter. 
- For Nov 2, E[D] ≈ -1.0°C. Assuming var ~ 20, standard deviation is ~4.5°C, yielding P(D > 0) ≈ 0.41.
- For Nov 25, E[D] ≈ -4.5°C. The standard deviation of the difference is ~5.3°C, yielding P(D > 0) ≈ 0.20.
- For Jan 24, E[D] ≈ -6.5°C. The standard deviation is ~5.7°C, yielding P(D > 0) ≈ 0.13.

Though traditional forecasting heuristics state uncertainty increases with length, the strong negative drift of the seasonal temperature cycle drives the expected values further away from the October baseline over time, meaning the true probability of exceeding the Oct 26 temperature becomes strictly lower for later dates.
